text: Iron is critical to optimal athletic performance due to its role in energy metabolism, oxygen transport, and acid-base balance.
Endurance athletes are at increased risk for suboptimal iron status due to the combination of increased iron needs and inadequate dietary intake.
Iron deficiency negatively affects both maximal and submaximal exercise performance in endurance athletes.
Exercise-associated inflammation and hepcidin release contribute to iron sequestration, increasing the risk of iron deficiency in endurance athletes.
Endurance athletes should be regularly screened for iron deficiency to prevent performance decrements.
Iron status in endurance athletes can be improved through diet modification or supplemental iron intake.
